gene_description: CD274 (Cluster of Differentiation 274), also known as PD-L1, encodes a ligand that binds to PD-1 on T cells, suppressing immune response. PD-L1 expression in tumor cells and immune infiltrate predicts response to anti-PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint inhibitors. Genetic variants in CD274 regulate PD-L1 expression levels.

source.title: Pembrolizumab for the treatment of non-small-cell lung cancer
source.finding: Pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1) showed improved survival over chemotherapy in NSCLC; PD-L1 expression ≥50% predicted superior response (KEYNOTE-001 trial)

CC.phenotype: Homozygous C allele associated with higher PD-L1 expression
CC.effect: Higher baseline PD-L1 expression in tumors; enhanced checkpoint inhibitor response predicted
CC.recommendation: Strong candidate for anti-PD-1/PD-L1 monotherapy. Monitor for immune-related adverse events. Consider combination with chemotherapy or targeted therapy depending on tumor type.

CG.effect: Intermediate PD-L1 expression; moderate checkpoint inhibitor response expected
CG.recommendation: Anti-PD-1/PD-L1 therapy indicated if PD-L1 IHC score ≥1%. Consider combination approaches for potentially enhanced efficacy.

GC.effect: Intermediate PD-L1 expression; moderate checkpoint inhibitor response expected
GC.recommendation: Anti-PD-1/PD-L1 therapy indicated if PD-L1 IHC score ≥1%. Consider combination approaches for potentially enhanced efficacy.

GG.phenotype: Homozygous G allele associated with lower PD-L1 expression
GG.effect: Lower baseline PD-L1 expression; reduced checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy response predicted
GG.recommendation: PD-L1 IHC testing essential. If PD-L1 <1%, consider combination chemotherapy+checkpoint inhibitor. Evaluate for other biomarkers (MSI, TMB).

clinical_significance: PD-L1 expression established as biomarker for checkpoint inhibitor response. CD274 genetic variants may modulate tumor PD-L1 expression and influence immunotherapy outcomes. Growing evidence for CD274 SNPs in predicting immunotherapy benefit.
